title: False `Warning-CASEWITHX` in case with wildcards and bad alternative syntax
body:
I accidentally used `|` instead of `,` as an alternative separator and got the following confusing message.
Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)
**Foo.sv**
```sv
`default_nettype none
`timescale 1ns/1ps

module Foo(
  input  wire logic [15:0] x,
  output      logic [15:0] y
);
  always_comb
  begin
    casez (x)
      16'h12?? | 16'h??21: y = 16'h22??;
      default: y = 16'hffff;
    endcase
  end
endmodule
```

**Output**
```
%Warning-CASEWITHX: Foo.sv:11:16: Use of x constant in casez statement, (perhaps intended ?/z in constant)
   13 |       16'h12?? | 16'h??21: y = 16'h22??;
      |                ^
                    ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/CASEWITHX?v=5.027
                    ... Use "/* verilator lint_off CASEWITHX */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
`verilator_bin.exe --lint-only -Wall  Foo.sv`

What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
`v5.027`
What OS and distribution are you using?
Windows, via oss-cad-suite

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
Not ATM.
(Please avoid attaching screenshots that show text - you can convert images to text using e.g. https://ocr.space)
